other_observers: Leland Flores, William Morris, Susan Allerton, Peter Rowland, Barbara Byron, John Breger, Toby Mourning, Andy Holland, Patt Dorsey, Jim White, Bob Watson, Pat Hayden, and many other memebers of nearby bird clubs.;
specific_location: 151 E. 16th St. Durango, CO 81301. Between the Colorado Division of Wildlife office and Visitor's Museum
description: Head black with orange supercilium. Throat orange with black breast band. Approximately 9 inches in length. Dark blue-gray mantle. Undertail coverts white. Median and greater coverts dark blue-gray on top, orange at tips. Wings orange and black. Tail dark blue-gray.Behaviors: Hopped around like an American Robin on the ground, picking up seeds. Very wary of any human activity. Flies into surrounding spruce trees when scared, and stays there until it feels safe.Call: Not heardPlumage: Adult male
